news.Title: Applied Materials makes unexpected AI move as demand explodes
news.Content:
Days before its fiscal second-quarter earnings report on May 14, Applied Materials ( AMAT ) unveiled a new co-innovation partnership with TSMC , the world's most important chipmaker, to be conducted inside Applied's new $5 billion EPIC Center in Silicon Valley.

It is the largest U.S. investment ever made in advanced semiconductor equipment research and development, and TSMC is now a founding partner.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. ( TSM ) is the world's largest dedicated chip foundry, with about 70% market share in 2025, according to Market Chameleon . Based in Hsinchu, Taiwan, it produces advanced chips for major tech companies like Apple, Nvidia , and Qualcomm, acting as a crucial, central node in the global artificial intelligence ( AI ) and electronics supply chain .

The timing is deliberate. AMAT is up 73.97% year to date and 189.52% over the past year, Yahoo Finance noted. Morgan Stanley raised its price target to $454 from $432 just last week, calling the upcoming print a "second leap in the right direction." This announcement, made days before the print, signals management has significant confidence in what comes next.

"Applied and TSMC share a long history of deep collaboration built on trust and a shared commitment to advancing innovation at the leading edge of semiconductor technology," said Applied Materials CEO Gary Dickerson .

What the Applied Materials and TSMC EPIC Center partnership actually means

The partnership is not a vague memorandum of understanding. It is a structured co-innovation program with defined technical objectives, conducted inside a physical facility designed from the ground up for this purpose.

The EPIC Center, which will be operationally ready this year, is designed to dramatically compress the time between early-stage semiconductor research and full-scale high-volume manufacturing.

TSMC, as a founding partner, gains earlier access to Applied's R&D portfolio and faster technology transfer cycles, advantages that matter enormously as chipmaking roadmaps advance at AI-driven speed.

Areas of technical focus for the EPIC Center partnership:

Process technologies enabling continuous power, performance, and area improvements across leading-edge logic nodes for AI and high-performance computing

New materials and next-generation equipment for complex 3D transistor and interconnect structures

Advanced process integration approaches to improve yield and reliability as devices move toward vertically stacked architectures

Source: Applied Materials and TSMC Partnership News Release

TSMC Executive Vice President Y.J. Mii framed the stakes directly. "Meeting the challenges of AI at a global scale requires industry-wide collaboration," Mii said. "Applied Materials' EPIC Center provides an ideal environment to accelerate equipment and process readiness for next-generation technologies."

My read of this partnership is that it gives Applied Materials something strategically valuable beyond the immediate revenue opportunity (multi-node visibility into TSMC's roadmap). That visibility guides Applied's own R&D investment priorities, a feedback loop that compounds over time.

Applied Materials heads into earnings with strong momentum, rising expectations

The TSMC partnership announcement lands against a backdrop of accelerating financial performance. In its fiscal first quarter of 2026, Applied Materials reported:

Revenue of $7.01 billion, down 2% year over year

GAAP EPS $2.54, up 75% year over year

Non-GAAP EPS of $2.38, flat year over year

Non-GAAP gross margin of 49.1%

GAAP gross margin 49%

Cash from operations of $1.69 billion

$702 million returned to shareholders through buybacks and dividends

Source: Applied Materials First-Quarter 2026 Results

For the fiscal second quarter 2026, Applied Materials guided for revenue of approximately $7.65 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $2.64, according to the AMT’s outlook .

Applied has beaten earnings estimates in each of the past four quarters with an average surprise of 5.24%, according to Zacks .

The question heading into May 14 is not whether AMAT beats. It is by how much, and whether the guidance for the July quarter matches the momentum Morgan Stanley is already modeling.

Why the AI semiconductor equipment cycle puts Applied Materials at the center

The demand environment driving all of this is not slowing. Applied Materials CFO Brice Hill said the company has "nearly doubled our system manufacturing capability" over the past several years in preparation for market growth. That is not the language you use unless you believe the cycle has significant runway.

The areas where Applied holds leadership — high-bandwidth memory DRAM, advanced packaging, and leading-edge logic — are precisely the segments where AI infrastructure spending is concentrating. Every new data center, every AI accelerator, every inference chip requires the manufacturing equipment that Applied Materials supplies. There is no shortcut around it.

AMAT traded at $447.27 on May 11, up 2.73% on the day, as of writing before close. The stock has returned 190% over the past year, compared with the S&P 500 's 31.13% gain, according to Yahoo Finance .

Three days from now, the earnings report will either confirm that the TSMC partnership and Morgan Stanley's "second leap" framing were perfectly timed, or set up the next chapter of a semiconductor equipment story that shows no signs of ending.

Related: Morgan Stanley adjusts Applied Materials stock price target pre-earnings

This story was originally published by TheStreet on May 11, 2026, where it first appeared in the Investing section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

For the fiscal second quarter 2026, Applied Materials guided for revenue of approximately $7.65 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $2.64.NurPhoto via Getty Images

For the fiscal second quarter 2026, Applied Materials guided for revenue of approximately $7.65 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $2.64.NurPhoto via Getty Images Applied Materials heads into earnings with strong momentum, rising expectations

news.Title: What Happens to Taiwan Semiconductor Stock if the AI Build-Out Slows Down? Here's My Answer.
news.Content: Few companies have benefited more from the artificial intelligence (AI) boom than Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM) . The Taiwan-based foundry fabricates the vast majority of the world's most advanced chips, including the graphics processing units (GPUs) and custom accelerators that hyperscalers like Microsoft and Alphabet use to train and run AI models. Up nearly 150% over the past year and trading at about $412 per share as of this writing, the stock ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) has significantly benefited from the AI boom, with its stock up nearly 150% in the past year.
• First-quarter results showed robust AI-driven demand, with HPC revenue growing 20% sequentially to 61% of total sales, and gross margin expanding to 66.2%.
• TSMC raised its full-year revenue growth outlook to over 30% and expects AI accelerator revenue to grow at a mid- to high-50% CAGR from 2024-2029.

news.Keywords: AI demand, semiconductor foundry, capital expenditure, hyperscaler spending, valuation risk

news.Title: Stocks Settle Higher on Strong Earnings
news.Content:
The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Monday closed up +0.19%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +0.19%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed up +0.29%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) rose +0.18%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) rose +0.28%.

Stock indexes settled higher on Monday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 10 posting new all-time highs amid strong corporate earnings results and resurgent optimism around artificial intelligence. Strength in chipmakers and AI-infrastructure stocks led the broader market higher on Monday. Gains in stocks were limited on Monday amid rising oil prices and bond yields after the US and Iran failed to reach terms to end the war in the Middle East. Global bond yields rose on concern that the continued standoff will keep energy prices elevated and could force the world’s central banks to tighten monetary policy. The 10-year T-note yield rose +5 bp to 4.41%.

More News from Barchart

Dear D-Wave Quantum Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for May 12

Berkshire Hathaway Just Upped Its Stake in Sumitomo Stock. Greg Abel Says It’s Holding for the Long Term.

This Analyst Just Raised the Price Target on Coherent Stock by 50%. What to Know.

Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

In the latest developments in the Middle East, President Trump and Iran rejected each other's latest peace proposals to end the 10-week conflict. Iran offered to transfer some of its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to a third country, but rejected the idea of dismantling its nuclear facilities. Iran also demanded a lifting of the US naval blockade and sanctions relief, while maintaining a degree of control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite the ceasefire in place since last month, a drone strike over the weekend set a cargo vessel ablaze off Qatar in the Persian Gulf. Also, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait both said they intercepted hostile drones.

Monday’s US economic news was slightly weaker than expected after Apr existing home sales rose +0.2% m/m to 4.02 million, below expectations of 4.05 million.

Chinese trade news was better than expected, a positive factor for global growth. China Apr exports rose +14.1% y/y, stronger than expectations of +8.4% y/y. Apr imports rose +25.3% y/y, stronger than expectations of 20.0% y/y.

WTI crude oil prices (CLM26) rose more than 2% on Monday, as optimism that the US and Iran would reopen the Strait of Hormuz was dashed after President Trump on Sunday said that Iran's latest peace proposals were "totally unacceptable." The strait remains essentially closed, as about a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas transits through the strait. Goldman Sachs estimates that the current disruption has drawn down nearly 500 million bbl from global crude stockpiles, with the drawdown potentially reaching 1 billion bbl by June.

Story Continues

The markets are discounting a 4% chance of a -25 bp FOMC rate cut at the next FOMC meeting on June 16-17.

Earnings reports thus far in this reporting season have been supportive of stocks. As of Monday, 83% of the 450 S&P 500 companies that reported Q1 earnings have beaten estimates. Q1 S&P 500 earnings are projected to climb +12% y/y, according to Bloomberg Intelligence. Stripping out the technology sector, Q1 earnings are projected to increase around +3%, the weakest in two years.

Overseas stock markets settled mixed on Monday. The Euro Stoxx 50 closed down -0.27%. China's Shanghai Composite rallied to a 10-year high and closed up +1.08%. Japan's Nikkei Stock Average fell from a record high and closed down -0.47%.

Interest Rates

June 10-year T-notes (ZNM6) on Monday closed down -11 ticks. The 10-year T-note yield rose +5.4 bp to 4.408%. T-notes were under pressure on Monday from a +2% jump in WTI crude oil prices, which boosted inflation expectations. T-notes fell to their lows on Monday afternoon on weak demand for the Treasury’s $58 billion auction of 3-year T-notes that had a bid-to-cover ratio of 2.54, well below the 10-auction average of 2.64.

European government bond yields moved higher on Monday. The 10-year German Bund yield rose +3.5 bp to 3.040%. The 10-year UK gilt yield rose +8.6 bp to 4.998%.

ECB Governing Council member Martin Kocher said, "If the situation around energy prices does not improve significantly, an interest rate hike will be unavoidable in the near future."

Swaps are discounting an 84% chance of a +25 bp ECB rate hike at its next policy meeting on June 11.

US Stock Movers

Chipmakers and AI-infrastructure stocks rose on Monday amid continued optimism over AI infrastructure build-outs. Qualcomm (QCOM) closed up more than +8% to lead gainers in the Nasdaq 100, and Western Digital (WDC) closed up by more than +7%. Also, Micron Technology (MU) and Seagate Technology Holdings Plc (STX) closed up more than +6%, and NXP Semiconductors NV (NXPI), Intel (INTC), and Texas Instruments (TXN) closed up more than +3%. In addition, Nvidia (NVDA), Applied Materials (AMAT), and Analog Devices (ADI) closed up more than +1%.

Mining stocks moved higher on Monday amid rallies in silver and copper prices. Hecla Mining (HL) closed up more than +11%, and Barrick Mining (B) closed up +9%. Also, Coeur Mining (CDE) closed up more than +6%, and Freeport McMoRan (FCX) closed up more than +4%. In addition, Newmont Corp (NEM) closed up more than +3%, and Anglogold Ashanti (AU) closed up more than +1%.

Consumer-exposed stocks retreated on Monday after Wells Fargo warned about weakening consumer demand. Kohl’s (KSS) closed down more than -10% and Dollar General (GD) closed down more than -8% to lead losers in the S&P 500. Also, Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings (OLLI) closed down more than -8% and Kontoor Brands (KTB) closed down more than -7%. In addition, Target (TGT) and Celsius Holdings (CELH) closed down more than -6%.

Airline stocks and cruise line operators were under pressure on Monday amid a +2% increase in WTI crude oil prices, which boosts fuel costs and undermines the companies' profitability prospects. American Airlines Group (AAL), Alaska Air Group (ALK), and Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd (RCL) closed down more than -4%. Also, Carnival (CCL) closed down more than -3%, and Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), United Airlines Holdings (UAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV), and Delta Air Lines (DAL) closed down more than -2%.

Beazer Homes USA Inc (BZH) closed up more than +34% on a report that said Dream Finders Homes is close to announcing a $704 million offer to acquire the company.

Babcock & Wilcox (BW) closed up more than +30% after reporting Q1 revenue grew 44% year-over-year, and that Q1 Ebitda nearly quadrupled.

Lumentum Holdings (LITE) closed up more than +16% to lead gainers in the S&P 500 after Nasdaq announced that the stock will replace CoStar Group in the Nasdaq 100 before the market opens on Monday, May 18.

Coherent Corp (COHR) closed up more than +13% on news that CEO Anderson will travel with President Trump to China this week.

Monday.com (MNDY) closed up more than +5% after reporting Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.15, better than the consensus of 93 cents, and raising its full-year revenue forecast to $1.466 billion to $1.474 billion from a previous forecast of $1.45 billion to $1.46 billion, better than the consensus of $1.46 billion.

Iren Ltd (IREN) closed down more than -10% after announcing that it intends to offer $2 billion of convertible senior notes due 2033 in a private offering.

Trade Desk (TTD) closed down more than -7% after HSBC downgraded the stock to reduce from hold with a price target of $20.

Wendy’s (WEN) closed down more than -7% after JPMorgan Chase downgraded the stock to underweight from neutral with a price target of $6.

Dell Technologies (DELL) closed down more than -5% after UBS downgraded the stock to neutral from buy.

Tyler Technologies (TYL) closed down more than -3% after announcing that it intends to offer $1 billion of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering.

Mosaic (MOS) closed down nearly -2% after forecasting Q2 phosphate sales of 1.4 million to 1.7 million tons, weaker than the consensus of 1.78 million tons.

Earnings Reports(5/12/2026)

Aramark (ARMK), Karman Holdings Inc (KRMN), Millicom International Cellular SA (TIGO), On Holding AG (ONON), Qnity Electronics Inc (Q), Ralliant Corp (RAL), Under Armour Inc (UAA), Zebra Technologies Corp (ZBRA).

news.Title: Why Sandisk (Not Micron) Could Be the Biggest Winner Of the AI Memory Era
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) memory boom has been kind to both Sandisk Corporation (NASDAQ: SNDK) and Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) . Sandisk shares are up about 560% year to date, while Micron has risen more than 180%. Micron sells the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) that pairs with Nvidia 's most expensive AI chips, along ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Sandisk Corporation (SNDK) shares are up approximately 560% year-to-date, while Micron Technology (MU) shares have risen over 180%.
• Both companies reported record revenue and significant margin expansion in their recent quarterly results.
• Sandisk's fiscal Q3 revenue rocketed to $5.95 billion (up 97% sequentially, 251% year-over-year), with non-GAAP gross margin expanding to 78.4%.
• Micron's fiscal Q2 revenue jumped 75% sequentially and 196% year-over-year to $23.86 billion, with adjusted EPS of $12.20.

news.Title: Cloudflare Stock Plunges Amid 20% Job Cuts. Executives Are Blaming AI.
news.Content: For years, Cloudflare (NET) became one of the internet’s most important gatekeepers, helping websites load faster, blocking cyber threats, and keeping digital traffic moving behind the scenes. But now, the company finds itself at the center of a very different conversation. And it's one that’s becoming impossible for the tech industry to ignore. The company announced plans to cut roughly 20% of its workforce, marking its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cloudflare announced significant layoffs, cutting approximately 20% of its workforce, its first major layoffs in 16 years.
• The company attributes the workforce reduction to a strategic shift towards an 'agentic AI-first' future, with internal AI usage surging over 600% in Q1.

news.Title: Stock market concentration - a feature, not a bug: McGeever
news.Content: By Jamie McGeever ORLANDO, Florida, May 11 (Reuters) - As the artificial intelligence boom accelerates, stock market concentration is reaching historic proportions – and not just in the U.S. where tech megacaps like Nvidia and Alphabet dominate. Increasingly, top-heavy indices are a feature of global equity markets, not a bug. More from Yahoo Scout What risks does AI-driven market concentration pose? Why might market concentration continue increasing globally? How does the passive concentration trap work? How concentrated are global stock markets becoming? The rise of the "Magnificent Seven" U.S. tech giants ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Market concentration is increasing globally, driven by the AI boom, with tech megacaps like Nvidia and Alphabet dominating.
• In Taiwan and South Korea, single stocks like TSMC and Samsung represent significant portions (around 40% and 20% respectively) of their benchmark indexes.
• This concentration can lift markets during upswings but poses risks if a few key companies' earnings or guidance fall short of high AI expectations.
• The 'passive concentration trap' describes how fund inflows disproportionately benefit the largest stocks, regardless of fundamentals.
• While top stocks' fundamentals are booming (e.g., top tech stocks accounted for 53% of S&P 500 returns last year), investors should scrutinize top-heavy index structures.
news.Keywords: market concentration, AI boom, tech megacaps, index weighting, earnings expectations

• 主動式台股ETF「主動統一升級50」(00403A)將於5月12日掛牌，成立規模達791.71億元，為台股ETF募集史上規模第二大。
• 該基金公布前十大成分股，台積電(2330)居首位，權重為11.69%，其他包括台光電(2383)、台達電(2308)等。
• 00403A結合市值型ETF與主動式選股特色，以市值前50大企業為基底，並從第51至200大企業中挑選。
• 選股策略聚焦成長前景佳且獲利能力優異的企業，以強化投資組合的成長動能。
• 統一投信繼00981A後推出00403A，顯示主動式ETF市場持續受矚目。
news.Keywords: 主動式ETF,成分股,台積電,統一投信,市值型ETF

news.Title: Intel stock extends rally as potential Apple chip deal fuels recent gains
news.Content: Investing.com -- Intel shares have risen a further 6.6% in premarket trading on Monday, extending a remarkable run that has seen the chipmaker gain 125.6% over ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel's stock has surged significantly, gaining 125.6% in the past month and 230.7% year-to-date, driven by a report of a preliminary chip manufacturing deal with Apple.
• The Wall Street Journal reported that Apple and Intel have finalized a formal agreement for Intel to produce chips for Apple devices after over a year of talks.
• Analysts view the potential deal positively, suggesting it could alleviate concerns about the sustainability of Intel's manufacturing services (IFS) and boost its stock.
• The deal is expected to benefit semiconductor equipment manufacturers like ASML, Lam Research, Applied Materials, and KLA, although significant spending increases are not anticipated before 2028.
news.Keywords: chip manufacturing, Apple deal, Intel IFS, semiconductor equipment, stock rally

news.Title: 台股質押潮湧現！券商爭取放寬借貸上限 證交所：2至3個月內提評估方案
news.Content: 台股站上 4 萬點，市場交投熱絡，投資人利用 ETF、股票質押再投入股市的現象日益普及，帶動券商不限用途借貸款項需求激增。民進黨立委李坤城 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股站上4萬點，市場交投熱絡，投資人利用ETF、股票質押再投入股市的現象普及。
• 券商公會建議放寬券商不限用途借貸款項總金額不得超過淨值4倍的規定，證交所預計2至3個月內提出評估方案。
• 金管會主委表示，融資上升反映市場熱度，業者已提出放寬建議，目前全體券商平均水位約1.6倍。
• 證交所董事長澄清，目前券商整體營運韌性與資金水位仍在安全範圍內，並未出現資金見底困境。
• 證交所要求券商落實KYC與風險控管，並將綜合考量風險控管等多重因素後提出具體方案。
news.Keywords: 股票質押,融資餘額,券商借貸,風險控管,證交所

news.Title: 買房不如買台積電更賺？首購族自備款全押股市　專家揭4大畸形警訊：恐掀本土金融風暴
news.Content: 台股今年市場氣氛熱絡， 年初登上3萬點，隨後僅花了約4個月的時間就攻破4萬點大關， ​ 今（11）日繼續上漲，不少人都將錢重押進股市。對此， 房市趨勢專家 李同榮 示警4大畸形現象，他直言，當前市場已出現 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 專家示警台股市場出現「全民瘋股」現象，連首購族也將購屋自備款轉投入股市。
• 此現象反映房市剛性需求延後、信貸資金過度集中股市、金融體系結構性失衡及市場投機氛圍升溫等四大警訊。
• 若股市在高槓桿後修正，可能引發股房雙崩及本土金融風暴。
• 若股市修正後資金轉向房市，可能重演過去炒房情況，形成資產泡沫。
• 專家認為當前市場投資情緒接近過熱，4萬點已是「九分飽」，4萬5千點恐過熱。
news.Keywords: 全民瘋股,股市泡沫,金融風險,房市,信貸

news.Title: The Semiconductor Market Could Double to More Than $1.5 Trillion by 2030. Here's the Best Chip Stock to Buy.
news.Content: U.S. hyperscalers came into the year planning to spend over $700 billion to build new data centers. Just three months later, most of them realized they would need to spend even more. Three of the four major hyperscalers increased their capital expenditure (capex) budgets for the year alongside their first-quarter earnings reports. And they may be just getting started. Leading contract chip manufacturer Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM) estimates the semiconductor market will reach $1.5 trillion ...
news.AI_Summary:
• U.S. hyperscalers are significantly increasing capital expenditure for data center construction, with plans to spend over $700 billion this year.
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) forecasts the semiconductor market to reach $1.5 trillion by 2030, a substantial increase from previous estimates.
• TSMC is identified as a long-term winner in the semiconductor market due to its leading manufacturing processes, advanced packaging capabilities, and significant capacity for advanced chips.
• TSMC expects strong revenue growth, exceeding 30% for the full year 2026, and maintains a long-term outlook of 25% annualized revenue growth between 2024 and 2029, with substantial capex investments planned.
news.Keywords: semiconductor market, data center capex, AI chips, manufacturing capacity, revenue growth

news.Title: Retail Floods Into ‘Silly’ Chipmaker Rally as Moves Get Extreme
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Retail traders largely sat out a record-setting advance in chip stocks in April. Now they’re diving in just as worries mount that the group’s rally may be losing steam. Most Read from Bloomberg Iran Makes New Offer on Uranium in Response to US, WSJ Says Trump Rejects New Iran Peace Offer as ‘Totally Unacceptable’ Inside a Year of Chaos and Conflict at Kevin Hart’s Media Company Drone Hits Ship Near Qatar as US Awaits Iran Peace Plan Response Judge to Review Musk, SEC Settlement Deal in Twitter ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Retail investors are increasing their participation in the chip stock rally, which has seen the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) surge by 60% in six weeks.
• Valuation metrics for chip stocks are considered stretched, raising concerns that the rally may be losing steam and posing a risk for late-entering retail investors.
• Analysts describe the current semiconductor rally as 'silly' and potentially 'parabolic,' with a high percentage of stocks trading above their long-term moving averages, indicating an overbought condition.
• While the AI boom supports structural arguments for higher valuations, the industry remains cyclical, and a moderation in demand is considered inevitable, though the timing is uncertain.
news.Keywords: retail investors, chip stock rally, valuation metrics, overbought, cyclical industry

news.Title: Alphabet considers first yen bond sale to fund AI goals
news.Content: TOKYO, May 11 (Reuters) - Alphabet plans to sell Japanese yen-denominated bonds for the first time, it disclosed in a filing on Monday, as technology giants ‌tap debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure deployments. The Google parent did not ‌disclose ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet is planning its first issuance of Japanese yen-denominated bonds to finance its artificial intelligence infrastructure investments.
• The offering is expected to be in the hundreds of billions of yen, with terms to be decided this month, managed by Mizuho, Bank of America, and Morgan Stanley.
• This move reflects a broader trend of Big Tech companies utilizing debt markets to fund costly AI ambitions, with total AI infrastructure spending projected to exceed $700 billion this year.
• Alphabet recently raised nearly $17 billion through bond sales in euros and Canadian dollars and increased its annual capital expenditure forecast.
news.Keywords: yen bond issuance, AI infrastructure, debt financing, capital expenditure, technology giants

news.Title: 不是聯發科、台達電！外資單週狂買1803億元，狂掃「鴻家軍」51萬張！散戶想跟單注意這件事
news.Content: AI熱潮持續升溫，全球資金再度大舉回流台股。根據台灣證券交易所最新統計，外資上週（5月5日至5月9日）在集中市場累計買超高達1803.98億元，不僅刷新近期紀錄，更成為今年以來單週第2大買超金 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 上週外資狂買台股1803.98億元，創今年單週第二大買超金額，帶動台股大漲6.88%。
• 外資買盤集中AI與電子權值股，前三大買超個股為群創(3481)、鴻海(2317)及台新新光金(2887)。
• 外資大幅加碼鴻海集團的群創與鴻海，顯示對其AI布局與供應鏈整合能力給予高評價。
• 台積電(2330)盤中出現獲利了結賣壓，但AI主軸仍撐盤，智邦、聯發科、群創等科技股維持強勢。
• 市場焦點將轉向基本面驗證，關注企業獲利能否跟上股價漲幅，以及AI供應鏈的實際接單與獲利成長動能。
news.Keywords: 外資買超,AI概念股,鴻海集團,權值股,基本面

news.Title: 台積電領軍！ 外媒揭5月最強3大投資標的
news.Content: 隨著人工智慧（AI）需求持續升溫，近期多家科技企業公布亮眼財報，也讓市場對AI產業鏈的成長預期再度升高。美國投資媒體《The Motley Fool》指出，在目前市場氛圍下，若投資人希望尋找兼具AI題材與長期成長潛力的標的 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 受惠於AI需求升溫，外媒推薦台積電(2330)為5月最強投資標的之一，因其與AI發展高度連動且具長期成長潛力。
• 台積電第一季營收年增41%，毛利率66.2%，其中高效能運算(HPC)業務季增20%，佔總營收61%，為主要成長來源。
• 隨著大型科技企業與雲端業者增加AI投資，台積電營運具成長空間，預估第二季營收年增35%。
• 儘管下半年可能面臨價格上升與擴張壓力，但擴產措施有助支撐未來需求，長線發展維持正向。
• 台積電作為AI浪潮下的核心受惠者，其財報表現常被視為市場前瞻指標。
news.Keywords: AI,台積電,高效能運算,營收成長,投資標的

news.Title: 台股土洋對作　外資調節緯創台積電
news.Content: （中央社記者何秀玲台北11日電）台股今天漲186.12點，收在41790.06點，成交值新台幣1兆1135.27億元；三大法人合計買 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股今日上漲186.12點收41790.06點，三大法人合計買超49.34億元，但外資已連2賣，賣超109.83億元。

news.Keywords: 土洋對作,外資賣超,AI概念股,類股輪動,財報

news.Title: 證交所AI×璞玉論壇5/18登場　打造台股雙引擎
news.Content: （中央社記者曾仁凱台北2026年05月11日電）台股頻創新高，惟資金朝AI科技股高度傾斜，證交所積極推廣璞玉指數，5月18日將舉辦「AI領航×璞玉發光」台灣資本 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 證交所將於5月18日舉辦「AI領航×璞玉發光」台灣資本市場成長論壇，旨在推動台股朝向更均衡且具韌性的雙引擎格局。
• 論壇將探討AI成長動能與璞玉企業穩健基礎的相互驅動，邀請謝金河、劉宗聖、李鴻基等進行對談。
• 證交所表示，AI產業需求升溫帶動台灣資本市場成長，顯示台灣在半導體與科技供應鏈中的關鍵地位。
• 台灣指數公司發布「台灣璞玉指數」，篩選具長期投資價值、穩健經營的中堅優質企業。
• 證交所將於5月20日起辦理「台灣璞玉指數」成分股公司業績發表會，並在COMPUTEX期間邀請AI產業公司分享。
news.Keywords: AI,璞玉指數,資本市場,雙引擎,論壇

news.Title: 美股收漲帶動台股今漲逾百點收4萬1　台積電大跌55元
news.Content: 【記者許麗珍／台北報導】美國4月非農就業數據優於市場預期，激勵美股4大指數收漲。台股今開高走高，收 ...

news.Content: The pace of first quarter earnings reports eases this week as economic data takes center stage , but investors will still be on the lookout for results from a handful of big corporations. So far this earnings season, S&P 500 companies have impressed market watchers by printing profits, even amid ongoing risks from the Iran war, and the index is on track for double-digit earnings growth. Reports from semiconductor companies last week ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The first quarter earnings season is winding down, with economic data taking center stage, but several key companies are still reporting.
• Semiconductor companies' recent reports have highlighted the ongoing impact of the artificial intelligence boom as a significant market driver.
• Companies scheduled to report earnings this week include Constellation Energy (CEG), Barrick Mining (B), Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), Plug Power (PLUG), Cisco Systems (CSCO), Alibaba Group (BABA), and Applied Materials (AMAT).

news.Title: Wall Street's Next Blockbuster Stock Split Was Just Announced -- and This Industry Titan Has Skyrocketed Over 51,000% in 32 Years
news.Content: For the better part of the last four years, artificial intelligence (AI) has been Wall Street's hottest trend. But it's not the only trend that has investors flocking to winning stocks. Stock-split euphoria has also played a key role in sending the broader market higher. In April, online travel leader Booking Holdings kicked things off by completing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• KLA Corp. (KLAC) announced a 10-for-1 forward stock split, effective after trading on June 11, aiming to improve share accessibility and liquidity.
• This move comes amid a strong performance for KLA, with shares having risen over 51,100% since 1994, driven by its dominant position in the semiconductor process control market.
• KLA's critical role in detecting defects in advanced chip manufacturing positions it well to benefit from the growth in artificial intelligence infrastructure.
• Despite strong fundamentals, the article notes a potential risk of an AI bubble burst, similar to past technology cycles, which could impact industry leaders.
news.Keywords: stock split, semiconductor process control, AI infrastructure, market share, technology cycle

news.Title: 台股上漲撐匯價　新台幣小升1.4分收31.415元
news.Content: （中央社記者潘姿羽台北11日電）美伊局勢持續僵持，美元指數重返98之上，不過台股上攻動能延續，為 新台幣 提供支撐 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 儘管美伊局勢僵持導致美元指數反彈，亞幣走勢分歧，新台幣匯率與台股、外資動向高度連動。

news.Title: Broadcom's Future $100 Billion Custom Chip Business Is Your Sign to Buy Now
news.Content: Few companies will go through as much of a transformation as Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) will over the next year. Currently, it's a large tech company with countless products under its roof, but one is about to become far more important than any other ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom (AVGO) is poised for significant transformation with its custom AI chip business, which is projected to become a $100 billion business next year.
• This projected growth represents a substantial increase from its current revenue, driven by increasing demand for application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs) tailored for AI workloads.
• While Nvidia (NVDA) dominates the general-purpose AI GPU market, custom ASICs offer greater cost-effectiveness for specific tasks like AI training and inference.
• The custom AI chip division reported 106% year-over-year growth in Q1, and management anticipates annual sales of custom AI chips to exceed $100 billion by the end of next year.
news.Keywords: custom AI chips, ASICs, revenue growth, semiconductor market, AI workloads

news.Title: Tech stocks today: AI chipmaker Cerebras to stage blockbuster IPO, Nvidia's Jensen Huang goes to China

news.Content:

news.Title: 台股4萬點全民投資熱 00981A單周圈粉10萬人

news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) remains the center of the artificial intelligence boom, but the stock is up just 15% in 2026, both because investors worry the current pace of AI spending is unsustainable and because they question the durability of Nvidia's dominance in the AI infrastructure market. Meanwhile, DigitalOcean (NYSE: DOCN) is a little-known cloud computing company whose aggressive expansion into AI services has led to tremendous shareholder returns. The stock is up 240% this year, and most ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Nvidia dominates the AI infrastructure market with its GPUs and vertically integrated AI systems, holding nearly 90% market share in AI accelerators and over 40% of AI data center spending.
• Nvidia's upcoming Vera Rubin platform is expected to deliver significant performance improvements, with Wall Street estimating 53% annual adjusted earnings growth through FY2028.

news.Keywords: AI services, cloud computing, market share, GPU performance, infrastructure investment

news.Title: 群創傳攜手台積電攻AI封裝　41萬張爆量攻漲停　友達同步漲逾5%
news.Content:
台股今（11）日早盤震盪後逐步走高，在多頭資金持續湧入下，加權指數盤中一度漲417.84點，最高來到42021.78點，連續第3個交易日盤中站上4萬2千點關卡。不過，尾盤受到權王台積電壓低影響，漲幅明顯收斂，終場上漲186.12點，收在41790.06點，成交值達新台幣1.11兆元。

籌碼面方面，三大法人合計買超49.34億元，其中外資賣超109.83億元，自營商買超37.29億元，投信則買超121.88億元。

群創傳攜手台積電布局先進封裝

面板大廠群創（3481）近期積極布局扇出型面板級封裝技術（FOPLP），繼打入低軌衛星大廠SpaceX供應鏈後，市場再傳出台積電也將與群創合作，攜手發展AI與高速運算相關的扇出型面板封裝技術。

雖然群創未正面回應市場傳聞，但董事長洪進揚昨（10）日在股東會年報「致股東報告書」中透露，群創半導體先進封裝技術已「站在全球領先群位置」，並在玻璃鑽孔（TGV）技術領域超前部署，且正與全球一線客戶合作開發技術，外界因此揣測與台積電合作傳聞有所關聯。

群創成交爆量攻漲停

利多消息激勵下，群創今天股價強勢攻上漲停，終場上漲2.9元或9.86%，收在32.3元，成交量達41.4萬張，高居今天台股成交量冠軍，還有23萬張排隊等買。

法人資金近期也持續回流群創，外資累計已連續7個交易日買超，累積買超約52萬張。

同為面板雙虎的友達（2409）今天也同步大漲1元或5.6%，收在18.85元，成交量31.6萬張，獲外資買超約31840張，排行台股成交量第三名。

聯發科、台達電撐盤

權值股今天呈現漲跌互見。台積電（2330）因市場傳蘋果部分訂單轉由英特爾（Intel）承接，股價開低走低，盤中一路下探，終場下跌55元或2.40%，收在2235元，單一檔拖累大盤約430點。

不過，聯發科（2454）與台達電（2308）表現亮眼，成為支撐指數的重要力量。聯發科漲250元或6.89%，收在3880元，貢獻大盤約132點；台達電上漲45元或2.05%，收2245元，貢獻約36點。

其餘權值股方面，鴻海（2317）上漲2元或0.8%、收252元；廣達（2382）漲3元或0.88%、收343.5元；日月光投控（3711）漲21元或4.07%，收537元。

記憶體族群全面噴發

受到美國記憶體大廠美光（Micron）與晟碟（SanDisk）股價大漲激勵，台股記憶體族群近期買盤火熱。

今天包括南亞科（2408）、華邦電（2344）、宜鼎（5289）、群聯（8299）、晶豪科（3006）等個股全面亮燈漲停。其中，群聯股價創下2670元歷史新高，南亞科則重返300元整數大關。

日月光砸148億買南科廠 群創連3賣業外收益大暴衝

智慧顯示大戰局》別再叫面板廠！友達、群創進擊2大新戰場

news.Title: 貸不了錢就把房賣了！ 他想All in台積電 專家嘆：連命都不要
news.Content:
台股近期站穩4萬點大關，AI熱潮持續延燒，護國神山台積電（2330）股價更一路狂飆，帶動市場投資情緒沸騰。不少投資人擔心錯過行情，甚至開始動用房產增貸、信貸進場加碼。有網友近日就在《台大批踢踢》發文透露，自己除了已辦理房屋增貸、身上還背有信貸外，甚至萌生「乾脆賣房All in台積電」的念頭，引發網友熱議。對此，房市專家阿明表示，人心不足蛇吞象，「用增貸去買股，就已經是賭了；賣房All in？那是連命都不要。」

房地產部落客「賣厝阿明」在 《賣厝阿明知識+》 粉專上表示，認為當前市場氣氛已接近「全民瘋股」狀態，許多人把投資當成穩賺不賠，但其實增貸買股本身就是一場高風險賭局，「你賭的不是股票會漲，而是賭它不會跌到讓你斷頭。」

阿明指出，許多人誤以為利用房屋增貸投資股票，是「拿銀行的錢賺錢」，但實際上，一旦股市反轉，投資虧損，貸款仍然必須照繳，房子也不會因為股票大跌就停止還款壓力。他直言，增貸已經算是高槓桿操作，而若進一步賣房投入股市，等同連最後的安全網都拆掉。

「至少增貸後，房子還在，還有地方住；賣房All in，一旦股市重挫，可能連住的地方都沒有。」阿明坦言，雖然這番話聽起來刺耳，但現實往往比市場熱潮更殘酷。「房子是你安身立命的所在，股票漲跌，你睡公園；房價漲跌，你至少還有一個家。」

針對近期市場投資氛圍，阿明也提醒投資人應建立風險控管觀念。他建議，若真的想參與股市行情，至少要替自己設下「停點」，當獲利達標時先拿回本金，避免過度貪婪，「用獲利繼續玩。虧到一定程度，就認賠出場，不要凹單」；同時也要保留退路，控制增貸額度在可承受範圍內，即便市場反轉，也不至於影響基本生活。

阿明最後強調，投資並不可怕，可怕的是失去理性。「人性就是貪，看到別人賺錢就想跟。但貪要有底線，底線就是你的家。連家都敢押上去，這不是勇敢，是憨膽。」他認為想參與市場，用增貸拿一部分錢出來，設好停點、控制風險，但不建議賣房All in股市。他說：「增貸已經是賭了，別連家都押下去。為自己留一條後路，別讓股票跌了之後，連睡覺的地方都沒有。」

（封面示意圖／翻攝Pexels）

news.Title: Amazon Prepares to Issue Its First Swiss Franc Bond in AI Push
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Amazon.com, Inc. is preparing to issue Swiss franc bonds for the first time, as Big Tech hyperscalers turn to new debt markets to fund artificial intelligence spending. Most Read from Bloomberg Iran Makes New ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon.com, Inc. is preparing to issue its first Swiss franc bonds to fund its significant investments in artificial intelligence infrastructure.
• Big Tech companies, including Amazon, Google (Alphabet), Meta, and Microsoft, are diversifying into new debt markets like Swiss francs and euros to raise capital for AI spending.
• Amazon has mandated several banks for a six-part Swiss franc bond sale across various maturities.

news.Keywords: AI spending, debt issuance, Swiss franc bonds, capital expenditure, tech hyperscalers

news.Title: 台灣模式赴美設產業園區　台積電盼原料設備供應鏈進駐
news.Content:
（中央社記者潘姿羽台北2026年05月11日電）台灣政府規劃以「台灣模式」與美國供應鏈合作，在美國打造產業園區。國發會主委葉俊顯今天表示，目前正就德州休士頓、亞利桑那州3處綜合評估，台積電也透露看法，希望進駐業者以上游原料、設備為主，但因牽涉化學品，法規問題尚待克服。

國發會主委葉俊顯身兼台積電法人董事身分，他日前與經濟部長龔明鑫共同參加美國商務部於華府主辦的「選擇美國投資高峰會」（SelectUSA InvestmentSummit），此次出訪也前往亞利桑那州視察台積電建廠預定地，並拜會州政府相關單位，續赴華府與美方高階官員及重要智庫進行交流。

葉俊顯表示，此次訪美其中一項任務是考察產業園區選址，目前評估德州休士頓以及亞利桑那州，後者已有晶圓代工龍頭台積電美國廠進駐，此行特別走訪亞利桑那州3處，一是台積電亞利桑那廠西北處，但距離較遠，二是台積電廠周圍，三是南邊的梅薩市（Mesa）。

葉俊顯表示，產業園區選址是從需求端的角度出發，此次也借重台灣區電機電子工業同業公會（TEEMA）海外開發經驗，就美國設立產業園區進行區位研究。

葉俊顯也說，產業園區牽涉範圍極廣，業者除了希望州政府出面協助，也期待與當地學校合作，其餘居住、教育、醫療等問題也須整體評估。

news.Title: If I Had to Start Over With $1,000 in Tech Stocks Today, Here's Exactly What I'd Buy

The tech sector has been the best way to obtain ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) is a top pick for tech stock investment due to its leadership in key future trends, particularly AI, where it is an AI pioneer with its Google Cloud, Gemini models, and integrated generative AI.
• Alphabet's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) are in high demand for AI applications, leading the company to sell them to external customers.
• Nvidia (NVDA) is highlighted for its dominant position in AI GPUs and its sticky CUDA software platform, with strong potential in autonomous driving and robotics.
• Apple (AAPL) is considered a strong business, though expected to have lower growth than Alphabet and Nvidia, with its ecosystem strength noted.
• The article suggests investing in fractional shares or affordable stocks to start with $1,000, focusing on the tech sector for market-beating returns.

news.Title: 台股42000點得而復失！台積電尾盤遭摜壓、聯發科股價續攻高
news.Content:
商傳媒 ｜記者顏康寧／台北報導

台股 11 日開高震盪走高，盤中一度攻上 42,021.78 點，挑戰 42,000 點整數關卡，不過尾盤在台積電股價遭摜壓下，指數漲幅明顯收斂。加權指數終場上漲 186.12 點，收在 41,790.06 點，漲幅 0.45%，成交金額約新台幣 1 兆 1,135 億元。盤面呈現權值股分歧、資金轉向中小型股與題材股的結構，顯示台股雖維持多頭氣勢，但高檔震盪與追價風險同步升高。

護國神山台積電成為壓抑大盤攻高的主要變數，台積電今日下跌 55 元，收在 2,235 元，跌幅約 2.4%，尾盤賣壓使大盤未能站穩 42,000 點。相較之下，聯發科延續強勢表現，上漲 250 元，收在 3,880 元，漲幅 6.89%，續創盤中與收盤新高。台達電上漲 45 元，收在 2,245 元；鴻海上漲 2 元，收在 252 元，成為撐盤要角之一。

從類股表現觀察，電子類股指數上漲 0.48%，金融類股指數下跌 0.72%，代表中小型股的櫃買指數大漲 3.07%。資金明顯從單一權值股轉向具題材性的中小型族群，包括機器人、記憶體、設備與部分高價電子股，盤面呈現「指數高檔震盪、個股輪動加速」的格局。

今日盤面焦點仍集中在 AI 供應鏈、半導體與高速運算相關族群。近期美股科技股與 AI 題材持續帶動市場風險偏好，台股在台積電、聯發科、鴻海、台達電等權值股牽動下，指數維持高檔；不過，隨著加權指數短線漲幅已大，市場追價力道開始出現分歧，部分資金選擇轉往櫃買市場與中小型題材股尋找補漲機會。

值得注意的是，台股今日雖然收紅，但未能站穩 42,000 點，代表整數關卡上方仍有獲利了結與解套賣壓。部分專家認為，AI 伺服器、先進製程、記憶體與機器人題材仍是台股中期主軸，只要國際資金持續回流亞洲科技股，台股多頭架構尚未明顯破壞。尤其聯發科強勢創高，顯示資金並未完全撤出電子股，而是從台積電等大型權值股，轉向具成長想像空間的 IC 設計與高階電子族群。

不過，也有分析師提醒，台股近期成交金額維持在兆元以上，代表市場熱度極高，也意味短線波動可能放大。若美國科技股回檔、美元利率預期反覆，或外資在高檔調節大型權值股，台股可能出現快速震盪。尤其指數連續挑戰 42,000 點未能站穩，短線投資人應留意追高風險。

本分析僅供參考，不代表任何投資建議。投資者應注意全球半導體產能分配波動、地緣政治風險及終端需求轉向之不確定性。市場有風險，投資需謹慎，入市前請獨立評估或諮詢專業顧問。

news.Title: 台積電CoWoS積極擴產仍跟不上！外媒：SK海力士評估導入英特爾EMIB 整合記憶體與ASIC
news.Content:

根 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 此舉旨在緩解台積電CoWoS產能緊缺的問題，並尋求封裝方案的多元化。
• 英特爾EMIB技術因其低成本、高擴展性及潛在的良率提升，被視為CoWoS的潛在替代方案。
• 多家科技巨頭如Google和Meta已將EMIB視為前景看好的選擇，英特爾預期先進封裝業務將帶來數十億美元營收。
news.Keywords: 先進封裝,EMIB,CoWoS,產能吃緊,供應鏈多元化

news.Content: 國發會主委葉俊顯今（11）日針對台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 赴美投資及周邊產業聚落（Ecosystem）佈局提出詳細說明。葉俊顯指出，台積電亞利桑那州一廠量產進度超乎預期，且 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 目標是在海外複製「台灣園區模式」，降低營運成本並強化台灣半導體供應鏈的全球韌性與競爭優勢。

news.Title: Palantir Just Showed Why Nvidia Stock Can Plunge on May 21
news.Content: Although the busiest week of earnings season is now in the rearview mirror, the most consequential report of the quarter is yet to come. Graphics processing unit (GPU) goliath Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) is slated to lift the hood on its fiscal first-quarter operating results (ending April 26) after the closing bell on Wednesday, May 20. While all ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Palantir Technologies (PLTR) reported strong Q1 2026 results with 85% revenue growth to $1.63 billion and raised its full-year sales guidance to 71%, yet its shares fell over 8% post-earnings.
• The decline in Palantir's stock despite strong performance is attributed to excessively high investor expectations and a persistent high price-to-sales (P/S) ratio above 60.
• Nvidia (NVDA) is expected to report strong Q1 earnings on May 20, driven by its leading GPU capabilities and sticky CUDA software ecosystem.
• However, the article warns that Nvidia could face a similar fate to Palantir if its growth fails to meet sky-high expectations, citing historical patterns of early-stage technology bubbles.
• Potential headwinds for Nvidia include the optimization timeline for AI hardware adoption by businesses and increasing internal competition from customers developing their own chips.
news.Keywords: earnings expectations, valuation risk, AI bubble, GPU market, competitive pressure

news.Content:

房產趨勢專家李同榮指出，「擦 ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Why BofA remains cautious on Intel stock despite ongoing rally
news.Content: Investing.com -- Bank of America raised its Intel stock price target to $96 from $56 but maintained its Underperform rating, arguing that a potential foundry deal with Apple, while significant, is already reflected in the stock’s current valuation. The Wall ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bank of America raised Intel's price target to $96 from $56 but maintained an Underperform rating, citing that a potential $10 billion foundry deal with Apple is already priced in.
• Intel shares surged 14% to a new all-time high of $124.92, extending year-to-date gains to nearly 240%.
• The deal, if finalized, could see Intel manufacturing ARM-based processors for Apple devices, potentially generating significant foundry sales by 2030.
• BofA analysts believe the deal's impact on margins and breakeven timelines is underestimated, suggesting potential delays in Intel's foundry breakeven target by one to two years.
• BofA favors AMD and ARM over Intel for benefiting from the rising server CPU market TAM.
news.Keywords: foundry, Apple deal, valuation, price target, server CPU

news.Title: Alphabet Plans Debut Yen Bond Sale as AI Race Accelerates
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Alphabet Inc. is planning to issue yen bonds for the first time in a move that may help fund investments as artificial intelligence competition intensifies. Most Read from Bloomberg Iran Makes New Offer on Uranium in Response to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet is planning its debut yen bond sale to fund investments amid intensifying AI competition.
• The company plans to increase capital expenditure to as much as $190 billion this year, up from $185 billion, to support AI infrastructure and compute capacity.
• Alphabet recently raised nearly $17 billion from its largest-ever euro-denominated bonds and debut Canadian dollar notes.
• Analysts note that Alphabet's total debt has ballooned to over $100 billion, but its ability to attract capital maintains significant financial flexibility.
• This move diversifies Alphabet's funding sources as it competes in the AI race and aims to potentially overtake Nvidia in market capitalization.

news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者劉居全報導】美系外資在新出爐的報告中表示，看好鴻勁(7769)將受惠台積電先 ...

news.Content: 問鼎三哥？千金股大洗牌。創意（3443）今（11）早股價衝上5730元漲停板價，超車川湖（2059）登上高價股第四寶座，甚至直逼鴻勁（7769）昨天收盤價5 ...

news.Title: 記憶體氣勢如虹！南亞科群聯等8檔亮漲停燈 分析師：不預設高點
news.Content:
（下午1時40分更新收盤）

國際記憶體巨頭持續走強，帶動台股資金今（11）日全面湧入記憶體概念股，南亞科（2408）、華邦電（2344）、群聯（8299）等多達8檔個股亮燈漲停，其中華 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 國際記憶體巨頭走強，帶動台股記憶體概念股，南亞科（2408）、華邦電（2344）、群聯（8299）等8檔亮燈漲停。
• 分析師認為，在AI需求推動下，記憶體已從修正整理重回主升段，行情具備延續性，不宜預設高點。
• 南亞科成功打入輝達供應鏈，進入AI伺服器記憶體供應體系，是重大轉機。
• 群聯高階企業級SSD獲利表現出色，推升族群股價。
• 國際記憶體大廠美光（Micron）與海力士（SK Hynix）股價強勢表現支撐看多趨勢。

news.Content:

The company successfully expanded into new areas such as high-speed interface applications and AI server CPU ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy in May
news.Content: Over the past few weeks, many artificial intelligence (AI) companies have reported strong growth, fueling renewed confidence and market highs. The S&P 500 is 8% as of this writing. If you're looking for excellent stocks to add to your portfolio to ride the wave higher, I recommend Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM) , Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) , and Lemonade (NYSE: LMND) . They all feature strong AI components, and they also have excellent long-term prospects beyond ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) reported Q1 2026 revenue growth of 41% year-over-year, with gross margin expanding to 66.2% and operating margin at 58.1%.
• AI is a key growth driver for TSMC, contributing 61% of total revenue through its high-performance computing segment, which grew 20% quarter-over-quarter.
• Amazon (AMZN) showed accelerated revenue growth in Q1, particularly in AWS, with its AI platform experiencing triple-digit revenue growth and a $20 billion run rate for its chips business.
• Amazon's advertising business grew 24% year-over-year, driven by AI improvements, and the company is expanding same-day shipping capabilities.
news.Keywords: AI, revenue growth, gross margin, operating margin, cloud services

news.Title: 華邦電產能利用率近滿載，訂單能見度看至2027年

華邦電（2344）第一季營運獲利表現顯著改善，DRAM與Fl ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 華邦電（2344）第一季獲利顯著改善，DRAM與Flash價格上漲是主要動能。
• DRAM位元出貨量季增約24%-26%，價格單季上漲約51%-53%；NAND Flash價格季增約34%-36%。
• 公司CSP客戶比重逐步提高，標準型記憶體在AI伺服器周邊應用需求上揚。
• 華邦電現有產能利用率接近滿載，訂單能見度已延伸至2027年，擬提升2026年資本支出至約405億元。
• 高雄廠月產能擬由15K提升至24K，台中Flash廠擴充測試與產線設備。
news.Keywords: 華邦電,記憶體,DRAM,NAND Flash,產能擴充

news.Title: 南亞科搶進高階LPDDR供應鏈， 4月營收創單月新高
news.Content:

南亞科（2408）傳出其LPDDR5/LPDDR5X產品通過驗證並列為輝達（NVIDIA）Ve ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 南亞科（2408）LPDDR5/LPDDR5X產品通過驗證，成為輝達（NVIDIA）Vera Rubin平台備援供應商。
• 公司產品組合由消費性DRAM為主，逐步向低功耗、高單價應用移轉。
• 南亞科4月合併營收創單月歷史新高，高階LPDDR及伺服器相關產品比例上升。
• AI伺服器對記憶體容量需求大幅增加，LPDDR比重提升可望改善產品組合與毛利結構。
• 國際大廠部分產能轉向HBM與先進DDR產品，造成LPDDR供給相對緊縮，提供南亞科進入高階應用機會。
news.Keywords: 南亞科,LPDDR,輝達,AI伺服器,營收新高

news.Title: 苟嘉章估NAND一路缺貨到2028年，明年三星HBM4E端具備強勁basedie優勢
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者李純君報導】上週六NAND控制晶片大廠慧榮，參與「家孩有我在」園遊會，會後總經理苟嘉章受訪時提到，NAND可能一路缺貨到2028年，而整體DRAM和NAND下半年價格都會漲，但幅度可能不及上半年大 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• AI發展驅動對高效能算力及記憶體容量需求激增，但記憶體產能擴充面臨土地、環保及設備等限制，預計將持續缺貨。
• Sandisk證實有客戶簽訂三到五年的長約，通常搭配預付款機制，有助於廠商現金流。
• 慧榮公司持續參與公益活動，關注失依兒議題，推動社會公益。
news.Keywords: NAND Flash,DRAM,HBM4E,記憶體缺貨,AI需求,三星,慧榮

news.Title: 台積電壓力來了？外媒爆蘋果轉向英特爾最大誘因：18A超便宜
news.Content: 蘋果過去完全仰賴台積電的局面將打破，《華爾街日報》披露，蘋果已與英特爾達成初步合作協議，未來將委託英特爾代工部分電子產品晶片，引發半導 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 外媒報導指出，蘋果已與英特爾達成初步合作協議，將委託英特爾代工部分電子產品晶片。
• 英特爾18A製程的晶圓價格據傳比台積電2奈米製程低約25%，對蘋果具吸引力，有助於降低成本並維持產品利潤。
• 此舉有助於蘋果分散供應鏈風險，並可能削弱台積電在高階製程的壟斷定價能力。
• 蘋果可能在2027年或2028年推出的入門版M系列晶片或非Pro版iPhone晶片中導入英特爾18A製程。
• 合作能否落地仍取決於英特爾18A製程的良率與穩定性，先前市場曾傳出良率僅在55%-65%。
news.Keywords: 蘋果,英特爾,台積電,晶圓代工,製程技術,成本考量,供應鏈風險

news.Title: Tesla vs. Rivian: Which Is the Better Growth Stock to Buy Now?
news.Content: Two of the main electric-vehicle stocks haven't had a great start to 2026. Year to date, shares of Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) are down about 13%, while Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ: RIVN) is off roughly 24% -- including a sharp drop on Friday after the smaller automaker's first-quarter earnings report. But could sentiment toward these two stocks improve? Now's a good time to look at both stocks, as each company has now delivered fresh first-quarter results. Additionally, with both stocks trading well below where they started the year, this is a good time to figure out which is the better growth stock ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Q1 revenue rose 16% year-over-year to $22.4 billion, with non-GAAP EPS jumping 52% to $0.41 and gross margin improving to 21.1%.
• Despite revenue growth, Tesla built 50,000 more vehicles than it sold, suggesting potential demand issues, and energy generation/storage revenue fell 12%.
• Capital expenditures for 2026 are now expected to exceed $25 billion, a significant increase, directed towards AI compute, Cybercab, and Robotaxi services.
• Rivian's Q1 revenue grew 11% to $1.38 billion, but it faces challenges with the R2 SUV launch price and expects a full-year adjusted EBITDA loss.
news.Keywords: revenue, gross margin, capital expenditures, EV market, autonomous driving

news.Title: 台積電掉單會影響台股嗎? 聯發科、記憶體、鴻海呢?
news.Content: 蘋果把訂單給英特爾甚至還有可能給三星打破台積電獨家代工局面，這也沒辦法台積電目前訂單已經滿到 2028 年產能處於極度緊的狀態，蘋果找尋第二供應鏈解決自身產能被排擠的風險也沒有什麼不對 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 蘋果將部分訂單轉移給英特爾或三星，打破台積電獨家代工局面，但因台積電產能已滿載至2028年，此舉被認為是蘋果尋求供應鏈多元化。
• 分析師認為此事件對台積電中長期股價影響不大，反而凸顯AI需求龐大，預期記憶體、PCB、被動元件等相關零組件將持續缺貨漲價。
• 預期台積電年底目標價不變，但認為股價約2300元時買進報酬率偏低，建議操作受惠AI漲價缺貨的記憶體、PCB、被動元件族群以賺取價差。
• 記憶體族群受惠於NAND市場供給吃緊及國際大廠投資新產線，預期將持續上漲，美光與SanDisk股價創歷史新高。
• 建議對台積電、聯發科、台達電、鴻海等權值AI股保持耐心，並透過操作節奏賺取價差。
news.Keywords: 晶圓代工,供應鏈多元化,AI需求,記憶體,價差操作

news.Title: 【台股4萬點】台股飆四成外資今年來提款4049億元 分析師：內資成定海神針
news.Content: 台股在AI狂潮推升下，台股今年以來漲幅超過4成，上周還數度挑戰42000點大關；不過，在台股創高之際，外資今年以來卻逆勢提款4049.7 ...

• 分析師指出，儘管外資賣超規模龐大，但ETF熱潮與內資買盤（如0050、00878、00929）的崛起，已大幅削弱外資對盤勢的主導力。
• 台積電四月營收為4107億元，為史上次高，上週股價曾貢獻股市超過千點。
• 聯發科因與谷歌合作提供ASIC設計服務，股價表現亮眼；記憶體族群受美光、SANDISK帶動，股價亦有表現。
• 分析師預期需關注6-8月除權息旺季外資是否會因領取股息再度匯出，但認為內資強勁承接力可望維持盤勢高位震盪。
news.Keywords: AI狂潮,外資賣超,內資買盤,ETF,營收

news.Title: 【台股4萬點】台股現泡沫疑慮？專家認「極短線過熱」：3警訊浮現
news.Content: 台股正式站上「傳說中」的4萬點，徹底打破去年底各大投信投顧的預期，不過漲得越高投資人往往越是緊張，深怕自己成了最後一棒。永誠投顧分析師藍 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 市場出現「巴菲特指標超標」等警訊，顯示市場可能過熱。
• 儘管有短線過熱疑慮，但考量台灣出口額增長、AI產業前景，台股仍具成長潛力，中長線仍有布局機會。
• 分析師提醒，目前警訊主要針對極短線，本益比雖走勢較前，但未進入真正危險區間。
news.Keywords: 過熱疑慮,乖離率,融資餘額,本益比,AI前景

news.Title: 【台股4萬點】風險大增？違約交割今年已出現第8起 券商雨天收傘
news.Content: 台股近期衝上4萬點大關，成交量也飆到兆元，AI概念股持續狂飆，市場另一頭卻也開始出現違約交割增加，券商收緊風控的現象。截至今年以來，上市櫃市場已出 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股近期衝上4萬點，成交量飆升至兆元，但違約交割案件增加，券商開始收緊風控。
• 今年以來已出現第8起違約交割案，其中穩懋累計金額達1.83億元，為今年上市櫃最大；金居累計約1.34億元，為第二大。
• 專家認為，高槓桿與當沖熱度升高是違約交割增加的主因，尤其在市場波動加劇時，高槓桿操作容易導致風險失衡。
• 券商限縮借貸額度，提高融券與借券門檻，被形容為「雨天收傘」，顯示市場風險意識升溫。
• 分析師認為違約交割多屬個人風控問題，對整體市場影響有限，但券商收緊額度可能在未來一個月左右對市場壓力慢慢浮現。
news.Keywords: 違約交割,券商風控,高槓桿,當沖,市場波動

news.Title: I Ran the Numbers on VONG, and These 2 Vanguard ETFs Came Out Ahead
news.Content: If you want to profit from the artificial intelligence (AI) boom, buying an ETF packed with growth stocks can be a good way to do it. One such popular fund is the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (NASDAQ: VONG) . This fund lets you own hundreds of stocks of large U.S. companies that are part of the Russell 1000 Index , including major ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) holds 387 stocks, with 59% in the technology sector, and its top five holdings (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, AVGO, AMZN) comprise nearly 43% of the fund.
• VONG has underperformed the Nasdaq-100 and barely outperformed the S&P 500 over the past year, suggesting a lack of diversification for investors seeking concentrated tech exposure.
• The Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT) offers exposure to 317 tech stocks, with semiconductors being the largest category (34.2%), and has delivered average annual returns of 24% over 10 years.
• The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) provides broader diversification across sectors like financials, communication services, and healthcare, with average annual returns of 15% over 10 years.

news.Title: Nvidia Reports Earnings in May. Here's Why I'm Loading Up Before the Report.
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) has been the most prominent investment theme on Wall Street over the past few years. Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has benefited dramatically from this trend, with the stock up over 640% in the past three years. But with its next earnings report on May 20, investors are concerned about whether the stock has already run too far or if there is still more upside left. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia guided for Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue of approximately $78 billion, implying 73%-80% year-over-year growth, with data center revenue at $62.3 billion in Q4 fiscal 2026 (up 75% YoY).
• The company anticipates at least $1 trillion of opportunity tied to its Blackwell and next-generation Rubin systems through 2027, driven by evolving AI demand for inference computing.
• Nvidia is investing up to $2.1 billion in data center operator Iren and providing multibillion-dollar prepayments to Corning to secure AI infrastructure capacity and supply chain components.
• While hyperscalers account for 60% of Nvidia's business, the remaining 40% comes from diverse sectors including enterprises, sovereign AI, robotics, and edge computing.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, revenue guidance, data center, inference computing, product cycle

news.Title: 買台股有如買台積…股市結構性風險難躲

news.Title: 2 Top Stocks Long-Term Investors Should Buy in May
news.Content: There is an old adage that says to "sell in May and go away." This is a strategy of selling stocks this month and then returning to the market in November after the summer, because there is a belief that stocks underperform during this period. While there is some merit to stocks underperforming during the summer months, this strategy has worked only 22 times in the last 53 years, and investors would have missed out on some big gains last year. The better move is to stay invested, and there are still some great buying opportunities out there. ...

news.Title: Could Buying Oklo Stock Today Set You Up for Life?
news.Content: Oklo (NYSE: OKLO) is designing a small nuclear reactor that could change how we power the world. Or, at least, how artificial intelligence (AI) gets fed. Indeed, with backing from Sam Altman and partnerships with several leading names in the AI data center space, Oklo is quickly emerging as a solution to one of AI's biggest bottlenecks: keeping servers running when ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Oklo is developing small nuclear reactors, positioning itself as a potential solution for the high energy demands of AI data centers.
• The nuclear energy industry, particularly small modular reactors (SMRs), is projected to grow significantly, potentially reaching $10 trillion by 2050.
• While Oklo has substantial long-term growth potential, its current valuation is high given it has no operating reactors and is still seeking regulatory approval.
news.Keywords: nuclear energy, AI data centers, small modular reactors (SMRs), regulatory approval, long-term growth

news.Title: Goldman Sachs resets Broadcom stock forecast
news.Content: Broadcom ( AVGO ) is a semiconductor giant with a vast product portfolio spanning wired infrastructure, wireless communications, enterprise storage, and industrial end markets. The company positioned itself as a major player in enterprise infrastructure software following its 2023 acquisition of VMware . The stock has gained 22.64% in the past month, according to Yahoo Finance at the time of writing, Saturday morning, May 9. Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index ( SPY ) is up 9.11% in the same period. Broadcom’s rally started ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom's stock has gained 22.64% in the past month, driven by extended partnerships with Google and Meta, and the launch of VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9.1.
• VCF 9.1 is designed for production AI workloads, offering significant cost reductions in server, storage, and Kubernetes operations for AI infrastructure.
• The company's revenue for the latest period was $19.3 billion, split between semiconductor solutions ($12.5 billion) and infrastructure software ($6.8 billion), with infrastructure software showing higher operating income.
news.Keywords: semiconductors, infrastructure software, AI workloads, VMware, partnerships

news.Title: Is Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing a Buy, Sell, or Hold in 2026?
news.Content: Even with all the chaos in the market this year so far, artificial intelligence (AI) stocks, both hardware and software, continue to surge to new highs. news.AI_Summary:

news.Keywords: semiconductor manufacturing, foundry, AI hardware, revenue growth, profit margin

news.Title: Palantir stock is cheaper than Alphabet on this valuation metric
news.Content: Two of the market's hottest artificial intelligence stocks have delivered jaw-dropping gains over the past three years . Palantir Technologies and Alphabet have both become investor darlings as the AI boom reshapes entire industries. While GOOGL stock is up 273% since May 2023, PLTR stock is up a whopping 1,340%. But with lofty price tags attached to both, a logical question emerges: which tech stock is the better buy right now? The answer depends on which measuring stick you use. And one particular metric flips the conventional wisdom on its head. Both ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) and Palantir (PLTR) are both high-growth AI stocks, but Alphabet trades at a significantly lower forward P/S (9.56x) and P/E (32.07x) ratio compared to Palantir.
• However, Palantir's market cap-to-free cash flow multiple (70.29x) is much lower than Alphabet's (335.01x), suggesting Alphabet's free cash flow is more expensive.
• Alphabet is aggressively investing in AI infrastructure, with Q1 2026 capital expenditures of $35.7 billion and projected full-year 2026 capex of $180-$190 billion, impacting its free cash flow.
news.Keywords: valuation, price-to-sales, price-to-earnings, free cash flow, capital expenditures

news.Title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet or Microsoft
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) stocks come in all varieties, but two of the biggest on the market are Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) and Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) . Both of these companies are known as AI hyperscalers, as they are rapidly building out the computing footprint necessary to handle a vast amount of AI workloads. This includes pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into capital expenditures to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet and Microsoft are leading AI hyperscalers investing heavily in data centers and AI workloads.
• In Q1 2026, Google Cloud revenue grew 63% year-over-year, outpacing Azure's 40% growth, though Google Cloud's figures include TPU sales.
• Alphabet's overall revenue growth was 22% with a 30% operating income increase, while Microsoft's revenue grew 18% with a 20% operating income increase, suggesting Alphabet had a stronger quarter.
news.Keywords: AI hyperscalers, cloud computing, Google Cloud, Azure, revenue growth

news.Title: Is It Too Late to Buy Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock After Its 12-Month Gain of 320%?
news.Content: Developing artificial intelligence (AI) software requires an astronomical amount of computing power, which is why most of it happens inside large, centralized data centers fitted with thousands of specialized chips called graphics processing units (GPUs). Nvidia leads the market for data center GPUs, but Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) is quickly catching up. AMD released its operating results for the first quarter of 2026 (ended March 28) on May 5, which revealed accelerating revenue growth led by the data center segment. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is rapidly gaining market share in data center GPUs, challenging Nvidia's dominance.
• AMD's data center revenue increased by a significant 57% in Q1 2026, contributing $5.8 billion to total revenue, with expectations of 80% compound annual growth through 2027.
• The company is launching its new MI450 series AI accelerators and Helios integrated data center rack, securing major deals with Meta Platforms and OpenAI.
news.Keywords: data center GPUs, AI accelerators, revenue growth, market share, semiconductor competition

news.Title: NVIDIA’s Huang says AI will make intelligence a commodity for billions
news.Content: Investing.com -- NVIDIA chief executive Jensen Huang said on Sunday that artificial intelligence will make intelligence itself a commodity accessible to anyone, from carpenters to shopkeepers, adding that the technology will reach billions of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang believes AI will democratize intelligence, making it accessible to billions globally and driving a new era of reindustrialization.
• He emphasized that AI development requires a broad range of skilled labor, not just engineers, for building chip factories and data centers.
• Huang stated that AI will elevate human potential by automating tasks rather than displacing workers, drawing parallels to past technological revolutions.
• NVIDIA's chips are foundational to AI data centers built by major tech companies including Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and Meta.
• Huang urged for responsible and optimistic engagement with AI, stressing the need for safety guardrails to keep pace with technological advancement.
news.Keywords: AI democratization, technological revolution, human potential, infrastructure buildout, responsible AI

news.Title: Where Will Amazon Stock Be in 10 Years?
news.Content: It might be difficult to envision holding on to one stock for 10 years. But long-term investing is ideal because it gives a company time to execute its business strategies while smoothing out short-term volatility and the impacts of temporary geopolitical crises or the business cycle. For Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) , the next decade will be crucial. The company appears to be at a crossroads, where it must decide if it wants to transition into a mature and stable business built around its currently reliable cash cow segments, or continue to pour money into ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon is leveraging its logistics network by opening it to other businesses through Amazon Supply Chain Services, aiming to diversify revenue and compete with UPS and FedEx.
• The company plans to spend $200 billion on AI-related capital expenditures, including data centers and custom AI chips, which could impact short-term stock performance if payoffs are not substantial.
• CEO Andy Jassy is optimistic that AI investments will reinvent customer experiences and eventually reward investors, similar to the early AWS infrastructure investments.
• Amazon's strategy focuses on pouring cash into new long-term growth drivers, particularly in logistics and generative AI, rather than solely on cost-cutting and shareholder returns.
news.Keywords: logistics, generative AI, capital expenditures, revenue diversification, long-term growth

news.Title: Are Small-Cap Stocks About to Make a Comeback in 2026?
news.Content: It's been a tough past three years for small-cap stocks. While they've not performed poorly, they've dramatically underperformed the massive AI-driven gains of the biggest S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) constituents. Since late 2022's bear market bottom, the S&P 600 Small Cap Index 's 55% gain has trailed its large-cap counterpart's 98% advance. As the old adage goes, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Small-cap stocks have underperformed large-cap stocks significantly over the past three years, with the S&P 600 Small Cap Index gaining 55% versus the S&P 500's 98%.
• Valuations suggest small caps are historically cheap (forward P/E of 16) compared to large caps (forward P/E of 21.7), with projected 2026 earnings growth of 17% for small caps.
• The dominance of AI favors larger companies that can afford to capitalize on the technology, hindering a small-cap resurgence.
• A potential catalyst for small caps could be increased difficulty in international business, which disproportionately benefits large caps due to overseas revenue.
news.Keywords: small-cap stocks, large-cap stocks, valuations, AI impact, market performance

news.Title: Prediction: AI Infrastructure Stocks Will Crush the S&P 500 in 2026
news.Content: Technology stocks, specifically the Magnificent Seven, powered the S&P 500 higher over the past three years. And though investors rotated out of some of these players earlier this year, that move may have been temporary. It's important to remember that the artificial intelligence (AI) story that drove the gains in recent years is far from over. In fact, it may just be getting started as AI today makes its way from the research lab into real-world applications. This year represents a key moment in the story as major tech companies pour investment into infrastructure build-out -- not to serve anticipated ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Major tech companies including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms plan to spend nearly $700 billion on AI infrastructure build-out in 2026 to meet current customer commitments.
• AI infrastructure, encompassing chips, servers, networking, and data centers, is expected to significantly outperform the S&P 500 in 2026.
• Demand for AI capacity is soaring, with companies like Amazon forecasting $200 billion in capital spending this year for AWS to monetize over the next two years.
• Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta contributed to S&P 500 earnings growth exceeding 27%, indicating strong performance driven by AI infrastructure investments.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, capital expenditure, data centers, revenue growth, market outperformance

news.Title: Berkshire CEO has sobering message for tech stock investors
news.Content: Berkshire Hathaway ( BRK.A , BRK.B ) CEO Greg Abel isn’t chasing the AI trade just because everyone else is. In his first annual meeting as Berkshire’s top honcho and Warren Buffett ’s successor, he said that the company isn’t treating tech spending or tech stocks as a momentum game. That’s the opposite of what we’ve seen transpire over the past couple of years, where the “Magnificent Seven” stocks have attracted billions in capital. Investors have been incessant in pouring money into tech giants pursuing their respective AI-first visions. AI leaders, including Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, are ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel expressed a cautious approach to AI investments, emphasizing building technology internally rather than just buying it.
• Berkshire is sitting on a record $397.4 billion cash pile but is prioritizing capital discipline and ensuring technology investments are additive to existing businesses.
• The company reported Q1 2026 earnings with operating earnings surging 18% to $11.35 billion, driven by GEICO and BNSF Railway.
• Abel's message contrasts with the recent market trend of pouring billions into tech giants like Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft for their AI initiatives.
news.Keywords: capital discipline, AI strategy, internal development, cash pile, operational earnings

news.Title: Is It Too Late to Buy Apple Stock?
news.Content: For much of 2026, Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) stock looked like a laggard. A drawdown earlier in the year had shares down by mid-single digits at one point in April. But sentiment has flipped quickly following the iPhone maker's recently posted record fiscal second-quarter results, which have helped the stock rally over the past month, putting shares at an all-time high on Friday. But has the rally already gone too far? Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple's stock has rallied to an all-time high following record fiscal second-quarter results, with total revenue up 17% year-over-year to $111.2 billion.
• Growth was broad-based, with iPhone revenue up 22% to nearly $57 billion, Services revenue hitting $31 billion (up 16%), and Greater China sales jumping 28% to $20.5 billion.
• Gross margin improved to 49.3% from 47.1%, leading to a 22% increase in earnings per share, demonstrating operating leverage.
• The company is increasing R&D spending by 34% to $11.4 billion, focusing on AI integration, including a multi-year deal with Google for Gemini models to power Siri.
news.Keywords: record earnings, revenue growth, AI integration, operating leverage, product momentum

news.Title: Broadcom Hits a Bottleneck as OpenAI Revenue Concerns Claim Their First Casualty
news.Content: The AI trade has been moving from one hardware bottleneck to the next, with many speculating where the next bottleneck could lie so they can lock in astronomical gains. What many fail to realize is that these bottlenecks raise a serious question for companies, namely, where to deploy their cash to gain a supply advantage over competitors. This makes the balance sheet itself a huge bottleneck, and Broadcom (AVGO) ...
news.AI_Summary:
• OpenAI's failure to meet new user and revenue targets has jeopardized its ability to finance the first phase of its $18 billion 'Project Nexus' deal with Broadcom for AI accelerators.
• This development raises concerns for Broadcom, as OpenAI's negative cash flows and missed targets make investors jittery, potentially impacting Broadcom's AI infrastructure buildup.
• Despite hyperscalers like Microsoft and Alphabet increasing capex, Microsoft might be unwilling to back OpenAI due to its financial performance.
• Broadcom reported solid Q1 FY26 revenue of $19.3 billion with a 77% gross margin, and expects Q2 revenue of about $22 billion, including $10.7 billion from AI chips, though these estimates may now be questioned.
news.Keywords: AI financing, revenue targets, cash flow concerns, supply chain bottleneck, semiconductor solutions

news.Title: 3 Stocks With Monster Potential to Hold Through the Next Decade of Uncertainty
news.Content: The world is uncertain today, with geopolitical conflicts unfolding across multiple regions. You should be very particular about which companies you invest in if you are a long-term investor looking to hold stocks for decades. news.Title: Are SanDisk and Micron Too Expensive? Here's How You Can Invest in the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory Supercycle for Just $50.
news.Content: The explosive growth of artificial intelligence (AI) is rewriting the rules for semiconductor demand, with memory and storage chips emerging as the next pillars deployed across data centers, model training, and real-time inference. Companies like Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) and SanDisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) have delivered jaw-dropping financial results and stock gains as AI systems become increasingly memory-hungry. Yet for many investors, these names look aggressively expensive after their respective run-ups. Will AI create the world's ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The explosive growth of AI is driving unprecedented demand for memory and storage chips from companies like Micron Technology (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK).
• Micron leads in High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI servers, while SanDisk benefits from NAND flash storage demand for AI data centers.
• Both companies are forecast to experience significant earnings growth due to AI demand being a structural supercycle, not a one-time spike.
• However, high valuations (P/E multiples) and volatility are concerns, with potential risks from hyperscaler capex slowdowns, increased chip supply, or pauses in model development.
news.Keywords: AI memory supercycle, HBM, NAND flash, valuation concerns, cyclicality

news.Title: Tesla Stock Is on a Winning Streak with China-Made EVs. Its Imports to Europe Are Increasing.

Toward the end of April, Tesla announced the beginning of high-volume ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Microsoft’s African data center falters on payment demands, Bloomberg News reports
news.Content: May 10 (Reuters) - A Microsoft data center site in East Africa has been delayed by disagreements with the ‌Kenyan government over the company’s request ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft's $1 billion data center project in East Africa, in partnership with G42, has been delayed due to payment disagreements with the Kenyan government.
• The project, announced in May 2024, aimed to expand cloud-computing services and utilize geothermal power.
• Microsoft and G42 requested guaranteed annual payments, which the Kenyan government could not fully meet at the requested level.
• While talks are ongoing and the project is not officially failed, the scale may be reduced.
• Discussions regarding power requirements and capacity structuring are still underway.
news.Keywords: data center, cloud computing, East Africa, government negotiations, project delay

news.Title: The Argument for Doing Nothing With Your Portfolio Right Now

news.AI_Summary:

news.Content: At the time of this writing, there's optimism that the U.S. and Iran may agree on a way to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the vital waterway that normally carries about 20% of the world's oil. However, oil prices remain elevated, and although stock markets have largely shaken off conflict-related concerns, the full effect of the blockage has ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Elevated oil prices are negatively impacting Bitcoin mining operations due to higher energy costs.
• Bitcoin's price is sensitive to inflation and consumer sentiment, both of which suffer from high oil prices.
• Inflation hit 3.3% in March, largely driven by energy costs, with average gasoline prices topping $4.50 a gallon.
• Despite tech optimism, high oil prices and potential Fed rate hikes reduce Bitcoin's appeal compared to safer investments like Treasury bonds.
news.Keywords: oil prices, Bitcoin mining, inflation, consumer sentiment, interest rates

news.Title: The Best Under-the-Radar AI Stocks to Buy in 2026
news.Content: You're certainly familiar with names like Nvidia and Palantir Technologies . The former remains the world's chief supplier of artificial intelligence (AI) data center processors, while the latter is one of the most-used decision-intelligence platforms. Both stocks have performed very well of late thanks to AI mania. The problem with stepping into such well-known names, however, is simply that these trades can be very crowded and therefore very expensive. As Warren Buffett famously advises , "You can't buy what is popular and do well." Oh, there are clear exceptions to his argument -- both Palantir and Nvidia continued rallying well ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article identifies under-the-radar AI stocks like Dell, ON Semiconductor, and Astera Labs as potential investments beyond well-known names like Nvidia and Palantir.
• Dell's AI Factory platform is gaining traction, with its infrastructure solutions group seeing 40% growth, contributing to a 19% increase in last fiscal year's revenue to $113.5 billion.
• ON Semiconductor provides critical components for AI-powered automation, including sensors and power controllers, and is partnering with Nvidia on 800-volt power solutions.
• Dell shares are trading 30% above Wall Street's consensus target, suggesting a potential need for a better entry point.
news.Keywords: AI stocks, under-the-radar, infrastructure solutions, automation, partnerships

news.Title: CoreWeave Stock Was Just Slammed. Is This a Buy-the-Dip Moment?
news.Content: Shares of CoreWeave (NASDAQ: CRWV) tumbled more than 11% on Friday, the day after the artificial intelligence (AI) cloud infrastructure specialist released its first-quarter results. The drop came after a torrid run for the stock; heading into earnings, shares had nearly doubled in 2026. What sent the stock lower wasn't the headline numbers, which on the surface looked transformational. Revenue more than doubled. The contracted revenue backlog approached $100 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• CoreWeave's stock fell over 11% after reporting Q1 revenue growth of 112% to $2.08 billion and a contracted revenue backlog of $99.4 billion.
• The market reacted negatively to light Q2 guidance, increased capital spending plans ($31 billion-$35 billion), and a widening net loss of $740 million.
• CoreWeave has secured new deals with Anthropic and an expanded $21 billion arrangement with Meta Platforms.
• The company's highly leveraged balance sheet, increasing share count, and lack of current profits make its valuation of approximately five times this year's revenue forecast a concern.

Will ...

news.Title: The Memory Shortage Is Coming for Big Tech's Bottom Line. This Company Will Hold Up Better Than Anyone Else.
news.Content: Memory prices continue to climb in 2026 as big tech's insatiable appetite for computing power grows faster than memory chipmakers can expand production. Samsung , the largest memory chipmaker in the world, says its pricing rose 90% in the first quarter. And big tech is happy to pay up for access to the chips that relieve one of the biggest bottlenecks in artificial intelligence (AI) training and inference. Meta Platforms , Alphabet , and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Rising memory chip prices in 2026 are impacting Big Tech's bottom line due to increased capital expenditures and depreciation expenses.
• Meta Platforms, Alphabet, and Microsoft cited higher component pricing as a reason for increased CapEx in their recent earnings calls.
• Apple faces challenges from high memory prices but is well-positioned to manage the impact due to its high gross margins and cash reserves.
• Apple's hardware gross margin was 38.7% in the last quarter, up from 35.9% year-over-year, but is expected to decline slightly next quarter due to memory pricing.
• Apple has options to absorb costs, enter long-term contracts, or potentially raise prices, while also aiming to increase market share.
news.Keywords: memory shortage, Big Tech, capital expenditures, gross margin, component pricing

news.Title: Prediction: The Helium Crunch Will Accelerate the Reshoring of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Manufacturing. Here Are the Best Growth Stocks to Own.
news.Content: Oil has been in the news since the start of the Iran war, but it's not the only natural resource facing disruptions. Qatar provides about a third of the global helium supply, and on Feb. 28, Iranian drone strikes hit QatarEnergy's Ras Laffan Industrial facility, one of only two in the world capable of producing semiconductor-grade helium. Although it appears the U.S. and Iran may be close to a peace agreement, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• A disruption in global helium supply, particularly from Qatar, poses a risk to semiconductor manufacturing.
• Reshoring of AI chip manufacturing is expected to accelerate due to supply chain vulnerabilities, benefiting U.S.-based foundries.
• Intel is positioned to benefit from reshoring, with its 18A process node in high-volume production and significant government support.
• Intel has secured deals with Tesla and plans collaboration with Alphabet's Google, with Apple also reportedly in talks for fabrication partnerships.
• TSMC is expanding its U.S. operations with significant investments in Arizona, aiming to benefit from the reshoring trend and maintain its leading foundry position.
news.Keywords: helium crunch, AI chip manufacturing, reshoring, semiconductor foundry, supply chain

news.Title: Wedbush sends blunt message on Elon Musk's Tesla and Intel deal
news.Content: Elon Musk has spent years talking about vertical integration across his companies. With Terafab, he is starting to put a price tag on it. A very large one. Wall Street is now watching closely. And Wedbush just told investors what it thinks the numbers actually mean for the companies involved. What Terafab is and why it matters Terafab is a planned semiconductor fabrication facility jointly developed by Tesla, SpaceX , and Intel, announced by Musk on March 21, 2026. The project is designed to produce chips for Tesla's self-driving systems, humanoid robots, AI data centers, and SpaceX's space-based computing infrastructure. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla, SpaceX, and Intel are jointly developing Terafab, a semiconductor fabrication facility with an initial investment of $55 billion, potentially reaching $119 billion.
• The facility will produce chips for Tesla's self-driving systems, robots, AI data centers, and SpaceX's space computing infrastructure.
• Terafab will utilize Intel's forthcoming 14A process, marking Intel's foundry business's first major outside commitment.
• Wedbush views the project as transformative for Intel, positioning it as a key player in next-generation U.S. semiconductor production, but cautions about execution risks.
• The long-term goal is massive wafer production (1 million per month) and custom chip manufacturing (100-200 billion annually) using 2-nanometer process technology.
news.Keywords: semiconductor fabrication, vertical integration, foundry business, capital investment, AI chips
