news.Title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Has Good News and Bad News for Nvidia Investors
news.Content: Few companies have benefited more from the artificial intelligence megatrend than Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) . The company has leveraged its market-leading GPU design capabilities to capture a massive share of the rapidly expanding AI compute market. It's built an entire ecosystem around its chips, ensuring it remains a key component of future data center buildouts. And Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) is one of its largest customers. The online retailer is also the largest public cloud ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon plans to spend approximately $200 billion on data centers and chips this year, an increase from $131.8 billion last year, with a significant portion allocated to Nvidia GPUs and ancillary chips.
• Amazon and Nvidia have agreed to a deal where Amazon will receive 1 million Nvidia GPUs by the end of 2027, expected to generate tens of billions in revenue for Nvidia over the next two years.
• Amazon Web Services (AWS) is increasing its use of its custom AI accelerator, Trainium, which is saving the company tens of billions in capital expenditures and differentiating its cloud offerings.
• AWS is making it easier for developers to use non-Nvidia AI accelerators through its Amazon Bedrock service, with a majority of Bedrock workloads running on Amazon's Trainium chips.
• Despite a large deal for Nvidia GPUs, Amazon is prioritizing its own Trainium chips, which are seeing significant demand and are projected to save the company substantial costs compared to using Nvidia's offerings.
news.Keywords: AI compute, data center buildouts, cloud computing, custom AI accelerators, supply chain

news.Title: Goldman Sachs spots a troubling big tech trend
news.Content: For years, investors in the biggest technology companies could count on one reliable outcome: the cash would come back to them. Profits piled up, balance sheets swelled, and buyback programs grew year after year, quietly supporting stock prices even when markets turned volatile. That dynamic is changing. A research note published May 7 by Goldman Sachs lays out exactly what is replacing it, and the implications stretch well beyond any single company or sector. AI spending is crowding out buybacks Goldman Sachs released a research note on May 7 identifying a major change in how the largest technology companies are ...
news.AI_Summary:
• A trend identified by Goldman Sachs shows that AI infrastructure spending is significantly squeezing share repurchase programs at major technology companies.
• The five largest AI hyperscalers (Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle) are expected to spend a combined $755 billion on capital expenditures in 2026, an 83% year-over-year increase.
• This massive capex commitment is expected to consume roughly 100% of their cash flows from operations, leaving little room for buybacks.
• Buybacks among these hyperscalers fell 64% year-over-year in the first quarter as cash was redirected to data centers and AI infrastructure.
• Goldman Sachs forecasts a rotation from buybacks to capex and R&D, potentially weakening the structural support that buybacks have provided to U.S. equities.
news.Keywords: AI spending, capital expenditures, share buybacks, cash flow allocation, tech sector trends

news.Title: 台積電小心了？傳英特爾搶下蘋果訂單 揭密黃仁勳、庫克下單「國家隊」內幕
news.Content: 全球半導體供應鏈傳出震撼彈！外媒披露，英特爾（Intel）與蘋果（Apple）已達成初步合作協議，有望承接蘋果部分裝置的晶片代工訂單。分析指出，若蘋果導入英特爾產能，有望降低對台積電單一供應鏈的過度依 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 外媒披露英特爾（Intel）與蘋果（Apple）已達成初步合作協議，有望承接蘋果部分裝置的晶片代工訂單。
• 此合作若屬實，將降低蘋果對台積電的依賴，並為英特爾晶圓代工服務（IFS）注入強心針。
• 此合作被視為美國政府重建本土供應鏈的關鍵成果，英特爾已與蘋果、輝達、馬斯克旗下企業建立合作關係。
• 蘋果因高階晶片供應不足影響出貨，正積極尋找第二供應來源，英特爾是目前唯一有能力快速擴張產能的企業。
• 受此消息激勵，英特爾股價周五（8日）暴漲13.93%，創歷史新高，顯示市場高度期待。
news.Keywords: 晶圓代工, 供應鏈, 蘋果, 英特爾, 先進製程

news.Title: The Evidence Is Piling Up: Nvidia's AI Chip Dominance May Be About to Come to an End
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has been one of the biggest beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence (AI) chip boom. Its graphics processing units (GPUs) are parallel processors, designed to break down certain types of massively complex calculations into a host of smaller parts, and then perform all of those small calculations simultaneously, rather than taking each task in sequence. And it turns out, the process of training large language models (LLMs) depends heavily on just the sort of tasks where GPUs excel. As a result, over the past few years, demand for Nvidia's industry-leading GPUs has skyrocketed, driving stunning growth in the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia's dominance in AI chips may be weakening as customers like Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta develop their own custom AI chips.
• Amazon's chip business hit a $20 billion annualized revenue run rate, with Trainium chips fully booked and $225 billion in purchase commitments.
• Google's TPUs are selling to more customers, with CEO Sundar Pichai seeing the TPU business as a key growth driver.
• Despite custom chip development, customers still rely on Nvidia GPUs for flexibility and universal compatibility across cloud providers.
news.Keywords: AI chip dominance, custom AI chips, GPU market share, TPU business, cloud provider flexibility

news.Title: The AI arms race is eating into Big Tech's cash flow: Chart of the Day
news.Content: The AI boom has made Big Tech richer, bigger, and suddenly much hungrier for cash. Alphabet ( GOOGL ), Microsoft ( MSFT ), Amazon ( AMZN ), and Meta ( META ) are all pouring more money into the physical backbone of AI — the data centers, chips, servers, and power-hungry ...

• Alphabet's forward price-to-free-cash-flow multiple has surged above 200x as investors pay up for AI upside while expected free cash flow is squeezed.
• Investors are concerned about how much cash they are willing to see reinvested before the payoff becomes obvious.
news.Keywords: Capital expenditures, AI infrastructure, cash flow, free cash flow, Big Tech spending

What Bernstein changed on AMD Bernstein upgraded Advanced Micro Devices to Outperform from Market Perform on May 6, raising its price target to $525 from $265. The $260 increase in the target is the largest single-note move on AMD from any major firm in recent memory. The ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bernstein upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to Outperform from Market Perform, raising its price target to $525 from $265.
• Bernstein sees AMD's Q1 results as 'very good,' with data center, client, and gaming segments exceeding expectations.
• The firm now sees AMD delivering more than $14 in EPS in 2027 and potentially approaching $20 in 2028.

news.Title: Are Amazon and Alphabet's Custom Chips a Threat to Nvidia?
news.Content: A few years ago, the primary argument in the AI computing space was whether Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) could maintain its dominance or whether a competitor like AMD could rise to steal market share. For the most part, Nvidia has maintained its dominance and has grown to own a far larger corner of the market than AMD. That argument has faded a bit, and a new one ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOGL) are developing custom AI chips that offer better cost-effectiveness for AI model training and running compared to GPUs.
• Amazon's chip business has a $20 billion annualized revenue run rate, with Trainium3 chips nearly sold out and Trainium4 chips 18 months away.
• Google's TPUs, developed with Broadcom, excel at inference and offer 80% better performance per dollar than the previous generation.
• Both companies remain committed Nvidia partners, recognizing the flexibility and universal compatibility of Nvidia GPUs.
news.Keywords: Custom AI chips, GPUs, cost-effectiveness, TPU business, Nvidia partnership

news.Title: Is Microsoft a good long-term investment in 2026?

According to Fortune , if you had invested $1,000 at the time of Microsoft’s IPO in 1986, you’d be sitting on roughly $5.5 million today. But despite Microsoft’s consistently strong earnings, double-digit growth, and massive cash flow, its future as a sure-fire investment is no longer written in stone, due to increased competition and its enormous bets on artificial intelligence ( AI ). Microsoft ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) is spending $190 billion in 2026 on AI data centers, chips, networking, and cloud capacity, causing operating expenses to rise.
• The company reported record revenue of $54.1 billion (up 29% YoY) and its AI business surpassed $37 billion in annual revenue (up 123% YoY).
• Microsoft has only 20 million paid Copilot subscribers out of over 1.4 billion Windows users.
• The company expects headcount to decrease year-over-year in 2026 due to ongoing operational evolution and agility efforts.

news.Title: Even if the Iran War Ends, These Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Stocks Face a Helium Problem That Isn't Going Away
news.Content: The past decade has seen an increase in global conflict. For investors, those tensions can mean short-term volatility and sector-specific disruptions. Historically, markets have recovered relatively quickly after geopolitical shocks -- which is one reason major indexes have set all-time highs in recent weeks, even as oil prices soar. However, some economists warn that markets are underestimating ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The war in Iran has hampered helium production and movement, a critical commodity for semiconductor manufacturing, particularly AI chips.
• Spot helium prices have doubled since the war's start, and shortages could take years to normalize, potentially causing chip manufacturing downtime.
• Qatar, a major helium supplier, has reduced gas production, and its helium exports are expected to fall by 14%.
• Semiconductor makers in Asia, especially South Korea, face challenges securing helium, but have secured alternative suppliers like Air Products and Linde.
news.Keywords: helium shortage, semiconductor manufacturing, AI chips, supply disruption, commodity prices

news.Title: Not Nvidia. Not Broadcom. Intel Is Going to Be the Biggest Winner of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Inference Era.

A lot of money has been spent on data center infrastructure that's optimized to train models using large datasets. Inference, on the other hand, doesn't require as much parallel-processing power as the training phase, as it needs to act on specific inputs to respond to queries in real time. news.Title: Tesla rare mistake prompts it to recall thousands of U.S. vehicles
news.Content: Tesla has built its brand on the idea that software is a strength, not a vulnerability. A recall confirmed May 6 is a reminder that the gap between those two things can close quickly. Orville Wright flew for 12 seconds and more than 120 feet at nearby Kill Devil Hills, but Kitty Hawk is forever known as the place where humans first took to the sky. news.Content:

news.AI_Summary:

• 第一季美股回跌主因是本益比修正，而非企業盈餘不如預期，顯示投資情緒影響大於基本面。
• 美國大型股的營業利潤率及EPS成長率優於其他國家，建議聚焦「美股、成長與大型」標的。
• 看好證券化商品及公司債，認為其利差具吸引力，有助於提供防禦價值。

news.Keywords: 經濟前景,股債投資,資產配置,企業盈餘,平衡型基金

news.Title: Tesla FSD hits major speedbump with EU regulators
news.Content: Last month, Tesla took the first step towards getting Full Self-Driving (Supervised) approved across the European Union, following the Netherlands' approval of its use on its streets. Getting a foot in the door was supposed to be the hardest part of the process, because once one of the economic bloc's members approves the technology, they can sponsor legislation to make that acceptance uniform across the 27 member countries. But Tesla FSD isn't going to get rubberstamped approval in Europe, as emails show skepticism from some European regulators. According to the emails, regulators from the Netherlands, Sweden, Finland, Denmark, and Norway ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) faces skepticism from some European regulators regarding speeding, icy road safety, and circumventing phone-use prevention.
• The Netherlands approved FSD use, and Tesla aims for uniform acceptance across the EU.
• The European version of FSD has stricter hands-on steering, limited driving profiles, and stricter eye-tracking requirements than the US version.
• Tesla needs approval from 55% of member states and 65% of their populations for FSD to be approved across the EU.
news.Keywords: Full Self-Driving, EU regulators, autonomous driving, vehicle safety, regulatory approval

news.Title: STMicroelectronics Touts LEO Boom: Sees Space Revenue Near €1B in 2026, €3B+ Through 2028
news.Content: Key Points ST projects it could be close to €1 billion in space revenue in 2026 and aims for well above €3 billion in cumulative space revenue across 2026–2028, with its LEO serviceable market forecast rising to roughly €2 billion by 2028 and near €3 billion by 2030. ST says LEO revenue jumped from about $175M in 2021 to ~$600M in 2025 (≈36% CAGR) and claims an RF market share above 90% in 2025, supported by a decade-plus co‑development with SpaceX that has delivered >7.5 billion ICs into millions of Starlink terminals and over 10,000 satellites. ST’s competitive edge rests ...
news.AI_Summary:
• STMicroelectronics (STM) targets over $3 billion in cumulative space revenue from 2026-2028, with LEO broadband electronics SAM projected at $2 billion by 2028.
• LEO revenue grew to ~$600 million in 2025 from ~$175 million in 2021 (36% CAGR), with STMicro holding over 90% RF market share in LEO.
• The company's decade-long partnership with SpaceX provides a competitive advantage in the rapidly expanding LEO market.
• STMicro cannot participate in China's satellite technology market due to export controls.
